entry.title: Planning to move to SAP S/4HANA Cloud? The CQC Transformation Assessment will help you succeed
entry.content:
Are you planning a move to SAP S/4HANA Cloud? Are you planning to deploy SAP Integrated Business Planning for Supply Chain? Excited about the possibilities and eager to make the right decisions early on? Looking to identify areas where your team's knowledge needs to be strengthened? Finding the right tools, services and learning resources from SAP can be time-consuming. This is where the continuous quality check (CQC) for transformation assessment can make a real difference.

“Continuous quality checks” are SAP Enterprise Support services that are available with every cloud subscription.

The CQC for transformation assessment will evaluate your project team’s understanding of SAP S/4HANA Cloud topics, SAP IBP topics, project challenges, and essential tools. It will help keep your project on track throughout the SAP Activate phases and provide personalized recommendations, including best practices and enablement resources.

 

An Example of Planning CQC Services for an SAP S/4HANA Private Edition Project*

(*Please note that not all available services are included)

 

An Example of Planning CQC Services for an SAP S/4HANA Public Edition Project*

(*Please note that not all available services are included)


An Example of Planning CQC Services for SAP Integrated Business Planning for Supply Chain* 

(*Please note that not all available services are included)


Key Benefits:

  • Avoid known and potential pitfalls early in your project phases.
  • Save time and effort by keeping your project team updated on the latest SAP tools and accelerators.
  • Learn from past projects to prevent known and potential pitfalls.
  • Receive personalized SAP best practice recommendations as needed.

🎥Watch our short video for an expert view on why you should avail of this service.

Check out the info sheets for detailed information on when and how to request it, and visit SAP Enterprise Support value maps for further guidance.


Get Started with SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps

Find the right content to accelerate the value of your solution with SAP Enterprise Support value maps. Included with every SAP cloud subscription, value maps offer structured, product-level Learning Rooms where you can navigate to curated content, be directed to self-service platforms, engage with fellow customers and SAP experts, and discover continuous quality checks, which can be found in the respective section of the value maps for SAP S/4HANA Cloud and Supply Chain Management solutions.

Access Value Maps in 3 Easy Steps:   

  1. One-time sign-up for SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, is required (please allow 2 hours process time)
  2. Access value maps 
  3. Navigate to the SAP Enterprise Support value maps for SAP S/4HANA Cloud and Supply Chain Management solutions. Once in the value maps, we welcome you to collaborate in our forums, ask questions or “Request a Call” to speak with one of our experts for further help. Value maps are easy to navigate but an on-boarding call allows us to tailor the conversation to suit your needs and highlight specific content for you. 

Got a question about the value maps, or our content?
Leverage the “ask questions” feature in the value map or contact us:
sapesvaluemaps@sap.com

entry.title: Revolutionized What's New Viewer Experience in SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
entry.content:
We have made an exciting new change in our What’s New Viewer for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. The viewer now lets you compare new features and changes between SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, and check which are available in both.

This is made possible because both products share a single What’s New Viewer. Your default scope selection will depend on where you access the What’s New Viewer from; either the SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition product pages on SAP Help Portal.

You can change the scope by using the options in the “Availability” filter. By selecting SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition you will view the features and changes that are available for both products. Selecting either SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition will filter to features and changes only available for that individual product.

We hope this update will give a broader understanding to both products, and support your daily What’s New Viewer experience.

entry.title: Shape the Future: Join the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 3, 2024!
entry.content:
We're excited to announce that the third cycle of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative for 2024 has begun, featuring 33 new projects designed to enhance innovation and collaboration for our valued SAP customers and partners.

In today's rapidly evolving business environment, staying ahead is crucial. The SAP Customer Engagement Initiative offers you a unique chance to gain early insights into SAP's product developments. By participating, you'll collaborate directly with our developers and product managers, influencing the future direction of our products.

Act quickly! Registration for the projects is open until November 15, 2024. Don’t miss this chance to make your voice heard and shape the next generation of SAP solutions.

To assist you in exploring the projects, we’ve created a detailed list for your convenience. Check out the full list here or follow the links below to discover the opportunities available:

SAP S/4HANA & SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  • Composable Enterprise: Reimagining Enterprise Software for Unprecedented Business Agility 
  • Help Where and When You Need It: User Assistance in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Improve your Post Upgrade Tests(PUT) Experience - An Integrated and Smart approach to PUT
  • Insightful Analytics for Actual Costing
  • Joule applications to SAP S/4HANA Utilities
  • Monitoring End to End Master Data Replications in SAP Cloud ALM
  • New Cost Settlement for real estate scenarios (Service Charge Settlement / CAM)
  • Purpose as base for processing of S/4HANA Business Partner personal data – concept and migration”
  • Research Project: Production Scheduling using AI
  • Research: SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit - AI: Field Mapping Proposal
  • Using AI to Extract Data from the Delivery Note to Update the Inbound Delivery

SAP Business Technology Platform

  • AI Agent Builder - A Low-Code Innovation with SAP Build
  • AI-Powered Autonomous Sandbox
  • Cloud Security: Improving the Encryption Key Management Experience
  • Discover SAP Build Process Automation Triggers: Empowering Experts to Automate and Extend Use Cases
  • GenAI Content Provenance and Authenticity via Watermarking
  • Investigation into a Customer Security Dashboard
  • Knowledge Graph RAG on unstructured Data

SAP Concur

  • Concur Expense - India GST handling
  • Concur Expense – Integrating Sales Opportunity Data with Concur Travel and Expense Data
  • Concur Expense - Preventing overspend through cost control, spend planning, and regulatory
  • Concur Expense - SAP Integration with Concur Solutions: Employee- and Benefit- focused Budget Management
  • Concur Expense – The Future of Expense Administration
  • Concur Travel - Trip Management / New Trip List
  • SAP Concur Solutions - Manage and track carbon consumption in T&E spend

Customer Experience

  • Redesigned SAP Sales Cloud, add-in for Microsoft Outlook

Data & Analytics

  • SAP HANA Database Migration to SAP HANA Cloud (XSC,XSA,CAP)

SAP Globalization

  • AI-supported Extension of Electronic Document Processing of SAP Document and Reporting Compliance
  • Localization as a Self-Service for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with Business AI
  • Sales and Use Tax Reporting in United States - User Research

Lifecycle Management

  • Business Service Management in SAP Cloud ALM operations
  • Clean Core / RISE with SAP Methodology Dashboard in SAP Cloud ALM

Others

  • Early Talent Management with SAP SuccessFactors

We believe that collaboration is essential for driving innovation. By partnering with our customers and partners, we can collectively shape the future of SAP’s products and revolutionize business operations.

So, mark your calendars, rally your team, and let’s embark on this journey together. Together, we can foster progress, and transform industries.

Don’t wait—register now at influence.sap.com and join the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 3 in 2024!

Warm regards,

Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: #05 - Understanding Project Methodology: The SAP Activate Methodology
entry.content:
The Importance of project management:

Effective project management is vital for delivering projects on time, within scope, and within budget. It provides structure and direction, helping teams navigate complex tasks and ensuring that everyone is aligned with the project's goals. Without a clear project management framework, projects can easily fall victim to scope creep, missed deadlines, and cost overruns.

Some common project management methodologies include:

  • Waterfall: A linear and sequential approach where each phase depends on the deliverables of the previous one.
  • Agile: An iterative approach that allows for flexibility and continuous improvement, often used in software development.
  • Scrum: A subset of Agile, focused on delivering smaller pieces of a project in "sprints" or time-boxed intervals.
  • Lean: A methodology that focuses on delivering maximum value to the customer by minimizing waste.

Each of these methodologies has its strengths, and the choice of methodology often depends on the nature of the project and the organization’s needs.

Introducing the SAP Activate Methodology:

At SAP, we’ve introduced a specialized project management methodology known as the SAP Activate Methodology. This methodology is designed specifically for SAP implementations and is tailored to meet the unique needs of businesses undergoing digital transformation.

What is the SAP Activate Methodology?

The SAP Activate Methodology is a simple, modular, and agile methodology used for implementing SAP solutions. It aims to accelerate implementations and drive continuous innovation by providing a standardized but flexible approach using best practices, agile principles, and a phased approach.

Key characteristics of the SAP Activate Methodology are:

  • Business Ownership: The business owns the solution, ensuring that the project aligns closely with business goals and delivers real value.
  • Lead with ‘Standard’ Best Practices: SAP Activate promotes using standard, pre-configured solutions that are based on SAP's best practices, minimizing the need for customization.
  • Agile, Modular, Scalable: The methodology supports an agile, scalable approach that can be adapted to different project sizes and complexities.
  • Rapid, Repeatable Delivery Steps: SAP Activate focuses on delivering value quickly and efficiently through repeatable steps.
  • Accelerators: Tools, templates, and content are provided to speed up the implementation process.
  • Quicker ROI: By using a fit-to-standard approach and best practices, organizations can achieve quicker return on investment.
  • SAP Solutions: designed to work with all SAP solutions, not just S/4HANA.
  • Six core project phases: Discover, Prepare, Explore, Realize, Deploy and Run.

Phases and main activities of the SAP Activate Methodology:

Access the SAP Activate Methodology tasks and accelerators via the SAP Roadmap Viewer:

The SAP Roadmap Viewer is a tool that provides access to SAP Activate Methodology implementation roadmaps. It offers a comprehensive view of project teams' activities, deliverables, and tasks associated with SAP implementations in in a structured way to support project planning and execution. It includes accelerator assets in document and hyperlink format to support implementation projects. The roadmaps are organized by logical categories or by SAP solution.

Recommendations for Success with the SAP Activate Methodology:

To fully leverage the SAP Activate Methodology, consider the following recommendations:

  • Learn about the SAP Activate Methodology: start with the Learning Journey Discovering SAP Activate Implementation Tools and Methodology to get a comprehensive understanding of how to manage your SAP projects using this methodology.

  • Join the SAP Activate Community: Engage with SAP Activate Methodology experts by joining the SAP Activate Community. This platform allows you to ask questions, share insights, and stay up-to-date with the latest news and developments in SAP Activate.

  • Explore the SAP Roadmap Viewer: The SAP Roadmap Viewer provides detailed guidance for your digital transformation journey. You can view and download SAP Activate Methodology assets and accelerators and even provide feedback to SAP.

  • Adapt to your project situation: While the SAP Activate Methodology provides a robust framework, it’s important to adapt the methodology to your specific project situation. Trust the process, but be flexible enough to make adjustments as needed to ensure the best outcomes.

Conclusion:

Understanding and implementing the right project methodology is crucial to the success of your SAP project. The SAP Activate Methodology offers a powerful, business-driven approach that aligns closely with best practices and the needs of modern businesses. By following the SAP Activate Methodology, engaging with the community, and utilizing available resources, you can ensure that your project is not only successful but also delivers rapid and sustainable value to your organization.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Check out other blogs from the series   Customer Onboarding at SAP
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

About the author:

  • Inês Moura supports with the design and orchestration of the customer onboarding journey for SAP RISE customers, ensuring a seamless experience from signature to successful go-live. As an Onboarding Advisor, Inês provides expert guidance and enablement, facilitating a smooth transition from the sales cycle to project start. With a focus on targeted project guidance and risk mitigation, Inês is dedicated to supporting SAP customers throughout the entire onboarding journey.

Having questions, comments or feedbacks? Don't hesitate to comment down below or contact @InesMoura or @Linh-Hoang .

entry.title: Moving SAP Screen Personas flavors from ECC to SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
We have been receiving some inquiries from customers who want to move their SAP Screen Personas flavors from ECC to SAP S/4HANA. I got the sense from the request that they were looking for some kind of wizard that would review the flavors, determine what field IDs changed, and update all the scripts accordingly. Since that does not exist, I’ll share some guidance on a process that will help you with your UX transformation as part of an SAP S/4HANA migration project. This applies when moving to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition or SAP S/4HANA (on-prem).

While an automated solution would be great, let’s consider the challenges of creating such a tool. There are thousands of classic apps in SAP S/4HANA. There are potentially even more ways that a customer might customize those apps, including creating custom fields, merging fields from different tabs within the same app, or integrating data across multiple transactions / apps.

As many technologists in Silicon Valley and around the world know, the perfect is the enemy of the good. So, rather than aiming for a 1-click fix that addresses every situation, we’ll discuss some ways for you to approach the process while, in parallel, we explore ways to use our inside knowledge of the transaction structure and field IDs to help find areas that might cause trouble with flavor migrations.

Let me start by stating the obvious. SAP ECC and SAP S/4HANA are very different products. Yes, they are both ERP systems, just as the Ford Model T and Ford Mustang Mach E are both vehicles that will help you address your transportation needs. SAP S/4HANA was engineered to leverage the strengths of an in-memory database to provide better and faster insights based on the real-time needs of modern enterprises.

SAP S/4HANA uses SAP Fiori as the main user experience

Another big difference is that SAP S/4HANA is designed to use SAP Fiori as the main user experience. Classic apps still exist in SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud and the option to use SAP Screen Personas or Adapt UI for Classic Apps remains if you want to simplify them to make them look and behave more like SAP Fiori apps. While some apps in SAP S/4HANA are like their predecessors in ECC, there are some factors to consider when thinking about moving your flavors during your upgrade.

1. Evaluate changes to process flows between ECC and SAP S/4HANA

There is generally not a 1:1 relationship between SAP Fiori apps and ECC transaction codes. In some cases, a single transaction will become several SAP Fiori apps as the model for SAP Fiori is to keep apps simple, focused, and role based. In other cases, several transactions might be consolidated into a single SAP Fiori app, if the app addresses a single business process for a specific role.

So, the first step before thinking about moving a flavor from ECC to SAP S/4HANA is to check whether an SAP Fiori app exists to accomplish the same task. If an SAP Fiori app exists, that will likely deliver a great out-of-the box experience with nothing custom to maintain. You can always adapt the app, if necessary.

2. Understand that classic applications evolve

If there’s no SAP Fiori app that does what you need, explore whether the corresponding Classic App in SAP S/4HANA introduced new functionality. Even if the assigned transaction code stays the same, the classic application could significantly differ between ECC and S/4HANA. For classic apps in SAP S/4HANA that add new capabilities, we recommend examining if the old flavor will leverage these new capabilities or if it is better to build a new flavor that exercises all the new stuff. If the new app and old transaction really are similar, we recommend thoroughly testing the flavor to ensure compatibility. 

3. Update themes to ensure visual harmonization in SAP S/4HANA

Flavors created for ECC systems likely use old SAP themes that generally require additional work to ensure that flavors look and perform with the latest SAP Fiori themes. This includes both functional and non-functional changes. For example, the next step in an SAP Fiori app is often accessed by clicking a button in the lower right corner. Classic apps have the next step in various places, so you would want to move “next”, “save”, or whatever to the lower right corner for visual consistency.
Another main difference in SAP Fiori themes is the header. Menu bar and toolbars are reorganized compared to old themes.  

4. Ensure that flavors work in My Home as a single-entry point

My Home is the starting point for SAP S/4HANA. You access your apps from there, whether they are assigned to you, you favorite them, or you accept the system recommendations based on your business role. My Home replaces the SAP Fiori launchpad, which looks rather drab in comparison.

Typically, flavors in ECC are accessed using the direct service URL. Using flavors in My Home is possible but requires some adoption to ensure flavors are correctly assigned to business roles and function correctly in the SAP Fiori launchpad service that launches flavors from My Home. 

5. Look at the best UI technology to meet your needs – they are always evolving

There are many technologies that might be part of your SAP Screen Personas deployment on ECC. Consider which ones make sense to use in the SAP S/4HANA context.

  • SAP GUI for HTML – this is the standard rendering engine for classic apps. If your flavors are optimized to run on SAP GUI for HTML, you are in good shape to migrate them.
  • SAP GUI for Windows - We don’t recommend using it with SAP Screen Personas due to lack of development and switch to Web technologies. Migrate your flavors to WebGUI if you want to use them in SAP S/4HANA.
  • Slipstream Engine – this was our recommended approach for building flavors for mobile use-cases. It is supported in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP S/4HANA on-prem, but not SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. The built-in SAP Screen Personas in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition that is accessed through the Adapt UI menu only runs on SAP GUI for HTML.
  • MOBGUI – for EWM_RFUI ITS Mobile replacement. This service has no title bar, toolbars, or menu bar, so if child controls are used in a flavor, they will not be available and a script button alternative must be used. For best results, we recommend that you use the latest versions of SAP Screen Personas and SAP S/4HANA (SP18 and 2023 FPS2, respectively, at the time of writing).
  • Web Dynpro ABAP – We recommend using options other than SAP Screen Personas to customize them, as explained in note 3239603.

6. Retire heavy custom development to follow clean core principles

Some customers use SAP Screen Personas to implement entirely new functionality. They create new screens that merge content from multiple transactions and then use complex scripts (1000+ lines in some cases) to perform updates to multiple transactions. Migrating such custom development from ECC to S/4HANA is not supported by SAP and would require extensive testing and maintenance effort on the customer side. If you want to create new functionality, we recommend building a custom app using SAP Fiori elements. The combination of SAP Build Code, SAP Fiori elements, SAP Fiori tools, and SAP Fiori AI simplifies the process and will provide a better result with significantly lower maintenance costs.

7. Reuse Control and Screen overwrite rules

If custom Control ID and Screen ID overwrite rules are used on ECC, they must be recreated on S/4HANA. Otherwise, Flavor changes will not be applied.

Summary (TL;DR)

  1. Evaluate changes to process flows between ECC and SAP S/4HANA
  2. Understand that classic applications evolve
  3. Update themes to ensure visual harmonization in SAP S/4HANA
  4. Ensure that flavors work in My Home as a single-entry point
  5. Look at the best UI technology to meet your needs – they are always evolving
  6. Retire heavy custom development to follow clean core principles
  7. Reuse Control and Screen overwrite rules

 

Good luck with your UX transformation projects. We have many resources to help you on your journey:

  • Take the SAP Learning course on using SAP Screen Personas with SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • Follow the SAP Learning course on developing apps with SAP Fiori elements
  • Subscribe to the SAP Fiori development newsletter

Thanks to Krists Magons, Tamas Hoznek, and Vladimirs Ananko for their contributions to this post.

entry.title: 2023 FPS02 What's New in SAP S/4HANA advanced Available-To-Promise (aATP)
entry.content:
With the 2023 FPS02 Release of SAP S/4HANA advanced Available-To-Promise (aATP), SAP has enhanced many existing capabilities for customers to meet complex business requirements in order promising, such as Third-Party Order Processing (TPOP) integrated via with Alternative-Based Confirmations, which enables the system to use supplies at the supplier for open quantities, after exhausting all alternatives in customer’s own network (TPOP in ABC).

With this release, we have also established a tighter integration of Business Process Scheduling (BPS) with Transportation Management (SAP TM) for delegation of transportation scheduling time. For customers who want to have full scope availability check from their external system (i.e. web-shop, e-commerce websites, etc), We have also enhanced our oDATA service for Simulative ATP check has been enhanced to include all basic functions like PAC, PAL, SUP, ABC and BPS. For more details each of these enhanced capabilities, check the following -

Third-Party Order Processing integrated with Alternative-Based Confirmations (TPOP in ABC) and other enhancements:

Leverage the advanced capabilities of third-party order processing in the context of alternative-based confirmations. This enables the system to either use the supplier as a full alternative with the full requested quantity or first check for the requested quantities within the own network (multiple plants), before triggering the purchasing from a supplier for the missing quantities, only.

Both TPOP-variants are supported:

  • “direct-ship” where the supplier delivers directly to the end customer, and
  • “bought-in” where the supplier first delivers to an internal plant, from where they are finally delivered to the end customer.

For both types scheduling scenarios can be modelled via BPS. Furthermore, integration with BOP and additional extensibility with new BAdI to modify the substitution results will be provided.

Tighter integration of Business Process Scheduling (BPS) with Transportation Management (SAP TM) and the ATP-explanation screen (RACR):

Leverage the SAP-Transportation management master-data and capabilities also in BPS by delegating the scheduling of a sequence of activities from BPS to embedded TM. This is supported by a predefined scheduling schema for a TM group call and via context specific visualization in the review availability check result screen (RACR).

 

Simplifying the Product Allocation (PAL) set-up, by …
… introducing dynamic time zone determination and
… displaying the consumed quantities on the PAL planning screens:

- With the dynamic time zone determination, the system automatically derives the time zone from the source location (or destination location) and uses this time zone in the allocation check to shift the time periods accordingly. This prevents that order items which are checked in different plants (or for different sold-to-parties) belonging to different time zones fall into different allocation periods, even if they are checked with the same local time.

- Allowing the user to see the currently consumed PAL-quantities directly next to the PAL planning data, drastically simplifies the manual maintenance and rework of the PAL planning data.

 

 

Further extensibility options and interfaces in form of new and enhanced BAdIs and/or OData-services:

Many areas of the advanced ATP have reached a decent level of maturity which allow them to expose their capabilities via OData services or manipulate their program flow or outcome via BAdIs. The most prominent ones in 2023 FPS2 are listed below:

  • The OData-service which executes a simulative availability check call, also support advanced capabilities now like Business Process Scheduling (BPS) and Alternative-Based Confirmation (ABC).
  • In the area of supply protection (SUP) multiple BAdIs have been introduced that allow to manipulate the protected quantities or even to remove complete restriction groups during runtime and therefore enable scenarios with dynamic protections.
  • The availability change log (ACL) is enabled to capture changes to any supply, demand or stock element on the granularity of Material, Plant, MRP Area, ACL Category, ATP Category and Checking Group. The new BadI can be used to manipulate the ACL-entry or even suppress it completely. Thus, follow up processes (like BOP-selections) can be influenced.

Enhancements in core capabilities like Product Availability Check (PAC), Supply Protection (SUP) and Alternative-Based Confirmation (ABC):

Increase reliability of the PAC-algorithm by virtually shifting or completely ignoring late supply elements depending on their delay and further characteristics like Checking Group, Document Purpose or ATP Category.

Support of Segments as Planning Level in Supply Protection

Increase flexibility in setting up the alternative-based confirmation by introducing
-…  hard constraints on the storage location
- … new options on how to create the remaining open quantity

Additional information on SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing for planning and scheduling and SAP S/4HANA advanced Available-To-Promise, including these new solution capabilities, planned roadmap items, and points of contact can be found here:

  • SAP S/4HANA advanced Available-To-Promise Roadmap
  • SAP S/4HANA advanced Available-To-Promise SAP Help
  • SAP S/4HANA advanced Available-To-Promise On SAP.com
  • Questions? Ask here

With all these enhanced features, SAP S/4HANA advanced ATP enables companies to manage their order promising in the order fulfillment process more effectively.

Please feel free to share your thoughts or feedback in the comment section.

Get in Touch with the Experts:

Product Marketing: Sujeet Acharya (sujeet.acharya@sap.com)
Product Management: Wolfram Schick (wolfram.schick@sap.com)

entry.title: SAP Sustainability Footprint Management: Q3-24 Updates & Highlights
entry.content:
In the past quarter, we continued delivering many more useful features and functions to SAP Sustainability Footprint Management and we achieved great success stories together with our customers. This blog post continues my quarterly series and gives you an overview of some of the highlights. I’m covering:

  • Calculate Footprints for Different Periodicities
  • Custom Emission Factors
  • Currency Conversion for Spend-based Calculation
  • Other Release Highlights
  • SAP Continuous Influence
  • Customer Success Stories

 

Calculate Footprints for Different Periodicities

We started SAP Sustainability Footprint Management with providing calculations of product and corporate footprints on a monthly level. Listening to feedback from our customers and partners, we have now also enabled the calculation for different periodicities, such as calendar years, quarters, and fiscal periods.

To use this new feature, first ensure that the correct user roles are applied. Next, you can import the required master data entities:

  • Periods – supporting calendar month, quarter, year, and fiscal period, quarter, year
  • Period Variants – specifying a classification, which can be a calendar or fiscal variant; the calendar period variant SAP_CY is delivered with the solution (for more information see Fiscal Year)
  • Ledgers – representing the central repositories for accounting data in SAP S/4HANA (for more information, see Ledger)

It's recommended to import ledgers and period variants before periods to ensure consistency. Master data is imported the usual ways via replication from integrated source systems, importing files, or APIs.

Now, you can configure the Footprint Inventory Scope by editing an existing or creating a new one. Choose fiscal year, period, or quarter, and fill the necessary ledger and period variant fields. For the periodicity to work, you need to activate form-based data collection and select value chain steps. Afterwards, the form-based data collection is enabled, and you can work with the Manage GHG Data Collections and Import Business Transactions apps.

Footprint Inventory Scope: Select Periodicity

In the Manage GHG Data Collections app, pick the period for which you want to collect data. A prefilled list of periods is available and filtered by periodicity and period variant, based on the previously selected footprint inventory scope. Via the Import Business Transactions app, import business transaction data by selecting the available footprint inventory scope and period.

When everything is set up, the footprint calculation can be created in the Calculate Footprints app. Select the same period as the ones selected when collecting and importing data. As usual, assign allocation schemes and energy flow models (if applicable) and choose a calculation variant. Note that those must be valid for the whole period. After filling out all information, the calculation can be triggered, and the footprint results can be viewed and analyzed in the respective analytical apps.

Manage Footprint Results: Calendar Year Periodicity

See the scenario guide Calculating Footprints for Fiscal Years/Quarters/Periods for all details and required steps. Accordingly, also the existing OData APIs available via SAP Business Accelerator Hub have been enhanced to support different periodicities.

 

Custom Emission Factors

We already provide many different possibilities to import emission factors from various sources and map them to business data replicated from your ERP system. However, it may be that there’s just no matching emission factor available, for example when you’re purchasing a complex product, manufactured from many different parts such as an engine. For those cases, we now provide the option to calculate a custom emission factor using the known emission factors of its individual parts or components. This custom emission factor aggregates the emissions data of all the items that make up the product or activity, providing a proxy or substitute for a direct emission factor. This method uses available data of existing datasets as reference, if imported previously or if those are part of the pre-delivered content, to estimate overall emissions.

Custom Emission Factor

In the Manage Emission Factors app, you find a new tab Custom Datasets where custom emission factor can be created by generating a custom dataset. This dataset aggregates emission factors from multiple component datasets. Essentially, the custom dataset serves as a data package and can be used as a reference for calculating emission footprints in consumer apps.

Begin by entering general information like what you would input in the EmissionDataset sheet of the import file, including the identifier, country/region, validity period, reference period, and reference quantity. Next, under Component Datasets, you can add a row for each component. It’s possible to either input manual values, where you provide the name, quantity, emission factor, and unit of measure, or you can select existing datasets as a reference. For the latter, you choose a dataset from a list, and only customize the quantity while other fields auto-populate based on the chosen data package. Proceed with filling out the remaining tabs, including classifications, attributes, qualities, and sources, corresponding to the information required for an imported dataset. After saving the new dataset, the application aggregates the emission factors of the component datasets and calculates the custom emission factor, displaying it in the header of the custom dataset under Custom Emission Factor. After releasing, the dataset is available as an emission factor reference in consumer apps, such as Manage Purchased Product Footprints. Released custom datasets can be managed similarly to data packages: You can undo a release, archive, restore, or delete them as needed. For more details and example use cases, see Create Custom Emission Factors.

 

Currency Conversion for Spend-based Calculation

Last quarter, we enhanced our calculation methods by introducing spend-based emission factors and calculations. We’re now improving this further and can now also handle multiple currencies. When the currency specified for a carbon footprint differs from that of the input item (such as a business transaction or activity), the system converts the currency using predefined exchange rates stored in the Currency master data and rounds the amounts to the defined decimal precision.

For example, given a box of strawberries costing 10 EUR with an exchange rate of 1 USD to 0,80 EUR, and a strawberry emission factor of 1 kgCO2e per USD, the system automatically converts the currencies and calculates a footprint of 12,5 kgCO2e for the 10 EUR box (10 EUR x 1kgCO2e / 0,80 EUR).

To use the feature, import the master data for the following new entities:

  • Exchange Rate Types
  • Exchange Rates
  • Exchange Rate Factors
  • Quotation Exchange Rates

Reimporting currency master data is recommended to update decimal precision. Otherwise, the system defaults to two decimal places. You can import the master data entities using the Import Master Data app or the Import Master Data (Corporate and Products) OData API on SAP Business Accelerator Hub which has been enhanced accordingly. The inverse method to convert amounts from one currency to another is also supported. In an exchange rate type, you can specify whether the system is allowed to use the inverted exchange rate. For more information, see Master Data Entities.

In the Record GHG Data app, when the period’s emission factor currency differs from the business activity’s currency, the converted amount is displayed automatically. The prerequisite is that currency conversion master data is available and that the amount can be converted into the target currency.

Currency Conversion in GHG Data Forms

 

Other Release Highlights

In the Manage Purchased Product Footprints app, you can now create a single footprint applicable to all plants in your master data by not selecting any specific plant. Previously, the system generated individual footprints for each plant, cluttering the released footprints list. This change streamlines and simplifies the process of creating footprints for purchased products and reduces clutter. Also, when leveraging the AI-based automatic mapping, now only one single footprint record will be created after releasing the mapping result, applicable to all plants. For more information, see Manage Purchased Product Footprints. To ensure that footprints are calculated correctly, a new default product determination method has been configured that is used for calculating a purchased product footprint based on the product and all plants combination. Check Manage Calculation Variants for more details.

A recent update to the Import Master Data app ensures that inconsistencies in Quantity Conversions, Quantity Dimensions, Global Trade Item Numbers, and Commodity Codes no longer affect the overall consistency of product master data records. This enhancement allows for product records with minor data issues to remain usable in further processes, improving the reliability and utility of the data. We also improved our documentation and the Resolving Data Inconsistencies section in Master Data Consistency has been updated with more information on this topic. For guidance on how the system validates units of measure in data records and how to resolve related inconsistencies across various data records, see Resolving Inconsistencies from Units of Measure.

An update of the Import Business Transactions app now allows the import of reversed business transactions, including corrections to previously posted goods movements and service confirmations. Unlike before, these reversals are no longer considered inconsistencies but are processed for footprint calculation. This enhances the accuracy of tracking carbon emissions associated with goods movements and resource usage. Reversals can either decrease or increase the footprint of products and ongoing production processes during the period they’re imported into SAP Sustainability Footprint Management. For more information, see Calculating Reversals of Business Transactions.

For providing direct feedback about your experience with SAP Sustainability Footprint Management, we implemented a new feedback tool, accessible via a new button on the launchpad. A survey will open in which you can rate your user experience and provide additional feedback or suggestions. This will help us to further improve the quality and features. Feedback is collected at a company level, and no personal data is captured. Please note that this tool isn’t intended for creating support cases or requesting technical support and you won't receive a reply regarding the feedback you submit.

Feedback Survey

The user interface of the application is now also available in additional languages:

  • Czech
  • Hungarian
  • Slovenian
  • Slovak
  • Romanian

Check SAP Help for the overview of all Supported Languages.

To help you better understand the application, we’re constantly improving the documentation on SAP Help. Recently, the help for the Manage Emission Factors app has been restructured and expanded, now including detailed information such as a list of consumer applications that utilize emission factor references, default data packages provided with the app, and an updated list of tasks. Additionally, new examples have been provided to clarify how calculation cycles work in processes where output becomes input in iterative cycles.

I talked about the integration of SAP Sustainability Footprint Management with SAP Sustainability Data Exchange in a couple of previous blog posts, enabling the sharing of actual footprints across the supply chain. You can obtain primary emissions from your suppliers to leverage in your calculation, and you can send the calculated results to your customers. This not only improves the accuracy of footprints and supports the move from average to actual data, but also strengthens the relationship with your customers. We’re happy to announce that SAP Sustainability Data Exchange is now generally available for all customers. Find more information in the feature SAP Sustainability Data Exchange Now Available to Help Companies Achieve Net-Zero Goals by Gunther Rothermel, as well as in this blog post by Leyi Liu: SAP Sustainability Data Exchange Launched as Generally Available.

Another crucial element is the integration of calculated footprints into SAP S/4HANA and your end-to-end processes to enable your business users making sustainable decisions. Besides out-of-the-box integrations in Purchasing and Inventory Management, you can also leverage Key User and Developer Extensibility to extend additional S/4HANA business processes with emission information. Besides the overall footprint, it’s now also possible to display the breakdown of the footprint into the individual GHG Scopes and categories directly in SAP S/4HANA. The blog post Key User Extensibility and Developer Extensibility for Carbon Footprint Category Breakdown by Martin Knechtel has all details and guides you through the required steps.

 

SAP Continuous Influence

At SAP, customer feedback is at the heart of our innovation process. With the SAP Continuous Influence program, you have the opportunity to directly impact the future development of SAP products and services. By sharing your ideas, needs, and priorities, you can help shape the direction of our solutions and ensure they meet your business needs. We’re excited to announce the launch of our new SAP Influence page for SAP Sustainability solutions.

Besides SAP Sustainability Footprint Management, this also contains SAP Sustainability Control Tower and SAP Responsible Design and Production. These solutions cover key sustainability topics like ESG-related disclosure, Material Transition, and Carbon Management. This dedicated platform allows you, as a customer or partner, to submit feature requests, vote on others' ideas, and receive timely feedback from SAP.

Check out the page at https://influence.sap.com/go/sustainability and start making your voice heard.

 

Customer Success Stories

With the Catena-X network, empowered by SAP Sustainability solutions, WITTE Automotive leads the way in supply chain decarbonization. The family-owned company was founded in 1899 and is a global leader in mechatronic locking systems for vehicles. They emphasize sustainability, climate protection, and circular economy considerations in their product development. WITTE needed a standardized method, widely accepted in the industry, to calculate and communicate their product carbon footprint (PCF). They also required efficient communication of PCF values to multiple customers and suppliers, instead of 1:1 interaction. To solve those challenges, WITTE implemented SAP Sustainability Footprint Management to calculate the PCF in compliance with the Catena-X rulebook, and significantly reduced the manual effort through automated data flow from their SAP S/4HANA system. They now further use SAP Sustainability Data Exchange to manage and share PCF data with business partners through the Catena-X network. As a result, through the ERP integration they achieve a 3-5 times more efficient calculation, compared to a manual, Excel-based method. They also save around 10k EUR per PCF calculation, compared to hiring external calculation experts. Read the full story here: WITTE Automotive customer story sustainability.

In my last quarter’s blog post, I already shared the SAP Innovation Award win by Martur Fompak and would like to provide some more details on their accomplishments in this edition. As one of the world’s leading automotive suppliers, they integrated SAP Sustainability Footprint Management with their SAP S/4HANA Cloud system to monitor their carbon footprint across core business processes. In combination with SAP Business AI, they can now make informed decisions based on the environmental impacts of different activities and components. The technology played a crucial role in a project for sustainable vehicle cockpit customization, enabling the development of an app using SAP Build Apps that allows users to personalize cockpit elements and choose sustainable materials. The app leverages SAP Business AI to suggest eco-friendly materials and uses virtual reality to showcase the environmental impact of choices. Additionally, Martur Fompak uses this solution for carbon footprint monitoring across 620 work centers and 100 energy analyzers, improving the transparency and efficiency of its energy consumption tracking. By doing so, they achieved significant environmental benefits, including a reduction of more than a third in car seat carbon emissions and over half in transportation emissions. The solution's detailed analytical reporting capabilities enable their employees and executives to incorporate sustainability data into daily decisions, such as adopting green steel and increasing renewable energy use. Carbon footprint calculations are now over 50 times faster compared to manual methods, and data collection is 75 times quicker than previous spreadsheet-based processes. This efficiency allows Martur Fompak to address sustainability challenges more swiftly and focus on strategic initiatives. By tracking emissions throughout product lifecycles, the company has met sustainability standards ahead of regulatory deadlines. Additionally, leveraging SAP Signavio Process Insights to optimize sustainability-related processes positions Martur Fompak as a leader in integrating sustainability with Cloud ERP systems. Ultimately, the company's focus on sustainability not only aligns with customer priorities but aims to effect industry-wide changes, encouraging vehicle manufacturers to adopt low-carbon solutions. Check out the full customer story: Martur Fompak: Unlocking sustainability with carbon footprint transparency. It’s also worth watching Özlem Altinisik, Martur Fompak’s Group Intelligent Technologies Director, telling their impressive story on stage at this year’s SAP Sapphire Barcelona Customer Keynote:

Another great customer is Matsumoto Precision, a producer of pneumatic control components and other automotive and machine parts, based in Japan. They’ve implemented SAP Sustainability Footprint Management successfully to calculate and report the carbon footprint of each of their purchased and manufactured products, providing essential environmental insights to business partners and setting a benchmark in sustainable practices. This supports them in their green transformation and aligns with the company's core strategy of creating ‘The Sustainable Factory’, which includes goals like self-sufficiency in electricity through solar power. Matsumoto Precision can now provide detailed carbon emission data for each product, adding value and differentiating their offerings in the marketplace. This initiative aims to drive a broader push towards sustainability within Japan's manufacturing sector, encouraging collaboration across various company sizes to improve national competitiveness. The project serves as a model for other companies looking to reduce their carbon footprints and contribute to a decarbonized society through collaborative efforts. Watch the following video to hear the CEO Toshitada Matsumoto sharing their story: Matsumoto Precision: Driving transformation to enable sustainability in the value chain.

 

Additional Information

For in-depth details on SAP Sustainability Footprint Management, including concepts, setup, and the application help, I recommend you browsing through the SAP Help Portal. For an overview of all new features and functions that have been released, check the What’s New section, which is updated with every new software release.

To get an outlook on the features and functions that are planned to be added to the solution in the next months, have a look into SAP Road Map Explorer. This is updated regularly and will provide you with an overview of all planned innovations and which benefits those will bring to you.

For exploring and experimenting in a live system, register for the free SAP Sustainability Footprint Management Trial. You’ll receive an e-mail with access information within seconds after your registration and can then test the solution at your own convenience within a 30-day trial period. It also includes a wide range of guided tours designed to help you navigate through all the features and functions comprehensively. Recently, we also added new tours and will shortly release additional ones, representing the latest features and an enhanced user experience.

If you want to learn more about the product, check out our SAP Learning Journey: Taking Action on Climate Change with SAP Sustainability Footprint Management. This eLearning includes three comprehensive courses, each consisting of multiple units. The training is interactive, featuring numerous hands-on simulations and exercises. Upon successful completion of the program, you’ll be rewarded with a Record of Achievement and a digital badge. In Q4-24, we will also deliver a major update to provide separate learning journeys for different roles and levels, stay tuned!

To stay up to date on SAP Sustainability Footprint Management, follow me here in the SAP Community. In regular cadence, I’ll publish blog posts giving you updates on all the product’s new features and functions as well as deep dives into the product capabilities. I’m also looking forward to engaging with you directly, feel free to leave your feedback in the comment section.

entry.title: What do we talk about when we talk about Clean Core in SAP S/4HANA for the Sales Processes?
entry.content:
Clean Core is a concept and philosophy supported by governance and guidelines that lay the foundation for a flexible and future-ready ERP. A Clean Core allows faster software deployment and easier adoption of SAP innovations. All our decisions must consider this, and to make it real, we must know the solutions, tools, and functionalities that we can use to follow this approach.

This blog post summarizes my journey focused on the SAP S/4HANA Sales area based on the Clean Core Concept.

Let’s follow the Clean Core Dimensions, the goals and how to achieve them in the Sales Area:

How can you reduce the complexity of your processes? If you are starting a new implementation, you can Leverage SAP_Best_Practices  or Enterprise Management Ledger for Multinational corporations.

SAP Signario Process Navigator helps to find best practices available in each SAP S/4HANA version, and you can filter by line of business and display all the items available. For example, the Best Practices for sales processes:

 

You can review the step-by-step process outlined in each Best Practice. These processes are ready to use. If they meet your business needs, you can adopt them as a starting point for designing your sales processes.

Additionally, with SAP Activate Methodology for RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition you can use the accelerators to speed up your implementation project.

When working on redesigning a process or planning to implement innovation in an existing system, it's beneficial to use the Best Practices as a baseline. From there, determine what is best for your business and set up the system accordingly.

In any case, it is important to have a governance structure with clearly defined roles and responsibilities to ensure adherence to the Clean Core philosophy during and after implementation.

 

Let’s discuss any missing business requirements in the Best Practice and how to add the extension while following the clean core concept.

First, Avoid extensions when possible. It is important to precisely understand the requirements before creating an extension. Take the necessary time to “look for” the actual requirement. If, after that, you conclude the extension is needed, follow one of the extensibility options that are part of the clean core principles:

  • In-app Extensibility: when you need a noncomplex new business feature with no risks.  Examples: UI adaptations, custom fields, custom logic, BADIs
  • Side-by-side Extensibility: when you need integration capabilities, like new UI or mobile applications, SAP or non-SAP external users of SAP S/4HANA data or accessing with logic.

In this blog, we will only focus on the "In-app Extensibility" option. So, how can you achieve these extensions while following a clean core concept in SAP S/4HANA Sales? Some examples are:

UI Adaptations: The Apps Variants feature can help you adapt your user interface in a simple way without any risk. You can watch an example in the SAP Fiori app Manage Sales Orders - Version 2: 

Custom Fields and Logics: Using the SAP Fiori apps Custom Fields and Custom Logic, you can include your own fields in your process in a compliance way, including the field in the standards tables. Also, in user interfaces like SAP Fiori apps and transactions, reports, e-mail and forms templates, BAPIs, APIs, and IDocs. Some business contexts where you can add your own field in SAP S/4HANA Sales are:

This blog post shows a step-by-step example: How to create custom fields in the Business Proces... - SAP Community.

Also, you can create your own logic in extension points grouped in a business context with the Custom Logic app. The business context available for SAP S/4HANA Sales:

You have a list of “Extension points” for each business context where you can include custom Logic.

 

For example, you can restrict the custom field property in the sales document. To do this, you can create your custom logic based on the Extension point: Custom Field Properties in Sales Document Headers ( ID: SD_SLS_FIELDPROP_HEAD ) in the “Sales: Sales Document” business context.

Please see in this video how to include a custom logic for the custom field 'ZZ1_TRACKSALES01_SDH' to hide in the sales document where the type of document is different as “OR”:

And the result:

Notice that you can create only one Extension point per ID. If you need additional logic, you can edit and add/modify the logic. This will ensure that all the logic is at the same extension point.

Data Lean optimizes the results of features that rely on AI, such as predictive SAP S/4HANA features. Considering the analysis is based on the data that fed the algorithm, the result will be as good as the quality of the data. 

Six data quality dimensions are crucial for ensuring that data is accurate, reliable, and useful for decision-making.

Accuracy             ->           Data should be free from errors and reflect the actual values.

Completeness   ->           No missing values in the data stored.

Consistency       ->           The data does not contradict itself when compared across different sources.

Timeliness         ->           The data should be up-to-date and available when needed.

Reliability           ->           Source data is known and proven to be dependable.

Relevance          ->           The data meets the needs of its intended use or users.

 

So, let’s think about some examples of how SAP S/4HANA Sales features could help the customer achieve some of these goals and also help maintain them over time.

 

The master data should be accurate and relevant. We know that any wrong data could cause a wrong condition or step in the sales process as well. S/4HANA offers, for example, a massive way to maintain and consolidate business partners.

Additionally, you can include SAP Master Data Governance for more functionalities.

Completeness: let’s talk about the SAP Fiori App - List Incomplete Sales Documents ( ID F2430 ). With this app, the customer could search for incomplete sales documents, display them according to the filter criteria, and edit and complete any of them. Note that the app title shows the number of incomplete sales documents. This can help the customer to notice it in a simple view:

In my experience in the sales area, consistency between the sales invoice and the financial invoice  sometimes not met due to erroneous accounting determinations. This scenario creates contradictions between the Sales and financial areas.

Situation handling is a tool that allows customers to notify the team of the situation that requires their attention. For billing documents not posted, there is a Situation template SD_BILLING_DOCUMENT_NOT_POSTED that can be used to inform the team responsible for noticing and resolving the issue and posting the billing document.

For more details regarding Situation Handling, you can follow this blog post: Uses Cases for Situation Handling in SAP S/4HANA S... - SAP Community.

These are just some examples of SAP S/4HANA Sales Area features that can assist the business in maintaining clean data. The business owns the data and its integrity.

Keep the landscape reliable and flexible ensuring seamless communication and data exchange between different systems or modules, minimizing errors and disruptions in the process.

Integrations must be based on standard APIs (OData and SOAP). The standard APIs to consume in the Sales Area as of SAP S/4HANA 2023 are:

 

  • APIs for Sales Force Support
    • Leads
    • Opportunities
    • Appointments
    • Phone Calls
    • Tasks
  • APIs for Order and Contract Management
    • Sales Master Data Management
    • Price Management
    • Sales Quotation Management
    • Sales Contract Management
    • Sales Order Management and Processing
    • Sales Billing
    • Claims, Returns, and Refund Management
  • APIs for Solution Business Management
    • Solution Quotation Management
    • Solution Quotation (A2X)
    • Events for Solution Quotation Management

You can explore each object here:  APIs for Sales | SAP Help Portal

Keeping operations effective and efficient, considering the end-to-end concept of operations, the latest release should be targeted, and housekeeping activities in line with best practices assigned to roles and responsibilities are part of this dimension.

Let’s focus on the end-to-end Sales operations.

Integration in the Sales area is crucial for a health operation: Sales Orders integrated with SAP products like SAP Field Service Management, integrations from billing documents with non-SAP products, master data replications with SAP or non-SAP products….as examples.

Some considerations to achieve this dimension:

  • Consider IT as a service to the business
  • Include into the integration alerts and monitoring
  • Establish a governance model for integrations regarding process, data, scalations

SAP Cloud ALM is a solution that provides monitoring and alerting for operations and gives continuity to the process. You can explore this feature and others for this solution here: SAP Cloud ALM.

We have come to the end of the blog, and I want to emphasize that the primary goal of the blog is to showcase examples of the key clean dimensions within the sales area.

I hope this was helpful for you 😊

I'm looking forward to your feedback and thoughts. You can post your questions in the SAP community, referring to this blog post.

Thank you,

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA RIG team.
